name: Old Tupi (Tupinambá)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY / CONTACT NODE (metadata only, not a modelled G2P target). Old Tupi (Tupinambá, the lingua franca 'língua geral' of colonial Brazil) is referenced only as the indigenous substrate/adstrate of Brazilian Portuguese (toponyms and lexicon: abacaxi, mandioca, capim, jacaré). No phonology is claimed; the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern). Glottolog tupi1273 (Tupinambá).

sources.author: Rodrigues, A. D.
sources.title: Evidence for Tupi-Guarani Genetic Relationship
sources.notes: Reference for Old Tupi / Tupí-Guaraní; cited as the indigenous contact layer of Brazilian Portuguese (structural node only).
